_comment: Spot registry. See docs/forecast-agent.md §2. Hand-maintained, never fetched.
_confidence: sourced = from a real surf source | partial = some fields unsourced | unconfirmed = spot itself is a judgement call. The code surfaces this; it must not be buried.
_disputedWindow: RESOLVED 2026-07-12, and not the way the prose reads. Surfline's spot-guide TEXT says Deep Hole/Trestles 'typically favor E swells'. Its own MACHINE-READABLE field for the same spot says optimal = [ESE, SE, SSE, S] = 101-191 deg, which EXCLUDES E. The prose is a loose regional label, not a bearing: Surfline applies the same 'E swell' phrase to Misquamicut, 25km inside the shadow where a literal 90deg ray is geometrically impossible. Surfline also reports swell direction from an open-ocean node ~41km offshore, OUTSIDE the Point Judith shadow -- so their 'E swell' is a deepwater direction, never the direction arriving at the reef. The raycast was right. 90-100 deg is blocked (by the shoreline you are standing on, which runs ESE toward the point). 105-135 deg is a TAPER, delivered only by diffraction -- and the exposure formula already produces that taper for free, gated on period. That period gate is our INFERENCE, not a sourced rule: no source anywhere states a period threshold for Matunuck. disputedWindow now marks the taper band, so the UI can caution on it.

spots.name: Narragansett Town Beach
spots.notes: Lacks shape, often closes out. Sunken barge + old pier near the North Pavilion. The small-swell default.

spots.name: Point Judith — East
spots.notes: The Aves + East of the Lighthouse. Rock bottom, rocks are the hazard. East/south face split is derived, not sourced.

spots.name: Point Judith — South
spots.notes: True point break, steep left and right; long lefts south of the lighthouse. Block Island notch at 195-205 is why the window is split. NE is offshore here and onshore at Monahan's — the defining contrast of this coast.

spots.name: K38 / K39
spots.notes: Mushy left, mostly fed through the breakwater gap. Size range unsourced.

spots.name: Matunuck
spots.notes: Covers Deep Hole, Trestles and The Point — three cobble reefs sharing one swell window (135-183, 197-215). Low tide matters: Deep Hole needs it, and all three get slow and fat at high. Boots year-round.

_merged: Deep Hole, Matunuck-Trestles and Matunuck-The Point were separate spots. They share an IDENTICAL open swell window -- same headland, same shadow -- and differ only in tide and period taste, which is finer than this uncalibrated model can honestly resolve. Merged into 'matunuck'. Green Hill, Monahan's Dock and Camp Cronin removed on the owner's call.
